hooks.command:
jq -nc --arg ctx "$(printf '=== Repo priming (auto-injected by SessionStart hook) ===

'; for f in HANDOFF.md README.md VISION.md FUTURE.md; do if [ -f "$f" ]; then printf -- '--- %s ---
' "$f"; cat "$f"; printf '
'; fi; done; printf '
=== Behavior reminder ===
After any non-trivial change in this session, pause and use judgement on whether HANDOFF.md (AI-relevant changes) and README.md (human-visible changes) need updating. Not every change needs both. Update on judgement only — do not auto-edit.

Destructive actions are gated by hooks (you cannot bypass):
- mcp__github__merge_pull_request triggers a harness approval prompt (Allow/Deny) on every call — announce intent in chat first so the user has context before clicking.
- git push to main/master is blocked — use a dev branch + PR.
- Compaction is blocked — stop and update HANDOFF.md, tell the user the session is full.
')" '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "SessionStart", additionalContext: $ctx}}'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"ask","permissionDecisionReason":"Merge requires explicit per-call approval. Review the PR number/title in the prompt and click Allow to merge this one PR, or Deny to block it."}}'

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'git[[:space:]]+push[^|;&]*[[:space:]:](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Direct push to main/master is blocked. Push to a dev branch and open a PR instead."}}'; fi

echo '{"continue":false,"stopReason":"Compaction is blocked by repo policy. Stop work. Update HANDOFF.md with the current state so the next session can resume cleanly, then tell the user the session is full and they should start a fresh one."}'

input=$(cat); active=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active'); if [ "$active" = "true" ]; then exit 0; fi; if ! git rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; changed=$( { git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null; } | sort -u); if [ -z "$changed" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$changed" | grep -qx 'HANDOFF.md'; then exit 0; fi; echo '{"decision":"block","reason":"Files changed this turn but HANDOFF.md was not updated. Add an entry capturing what changed and why anything a future AI session would need to know that is not already obvious from the diff. If the change is genuinely too trivial to log usefully (e.g., a typo fix), add a one-line entry that says exactly that. Do not pad with vague summaries like misc updates."}'
